summary: @variational/common-ui 1.1.0 exfiltrates environment secrets and opens a reverse shell on import
details: On require()/import, lib/index.js schedules a setTimeout that performs two attacker-controlled actions against the installer's host. First, it harvests environment variables whose names match /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i, runs `id`/`whoami`, reads `/proc/1/environ` (container init env), and collects hostname/username/cwd, then POSTs the JSON blob over HTTP to vexar-space.org/api/telemetry. Second, it opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:8082 and pipes a `/bin/sh` child process's stdin/stdout/stderr through it, yielding an interactive remote shell. The destination host, port, path, `/bin/sh` literal, and the `require()` strings for http/os/child_process/net are charcode-obfuscated to hide the indicators. A small set of legitimate-looking exports (ABORT_REASON_*, createPollerConfig) ships alongside the payload as a cover-story API. Any developer machine, CI runner, or build container that imports this package is compromised: credentials in env are exfiltrated and the attacker obtains shell access.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
